data.title: Emergent BioSolutions discloses CEO stock purchase
data.description:
• Emergent BioSolutions (NYSE:EBS) revealed that its chief executive officer, Joseph C. Papa, bought shares of the company worth about $350,000.
  • The stock is up about 15% in morning trade.
  • The CEO bought 60,000 shares at a price of $5.83 per share.  Papa now holds about 342,500 shares of the company now.

data.title: Medallion Financial announces pricing of 3M series G preferred stock offering
data.description:
Medallion Financial (NASDAQ:MFIN) announced on Friday the pricing of a public offering of 3M shares of its fixed-rate reset non-cumulative perpetual preferred stock, series G.

The preferred stock carries a liquidation amount of $25 per share and an aggregate liquidation amount of $75M.

The dividends will accrue on the liquidation amount of $25 per share at a fixed rate per annum equal to 9.00% from the original issue date to July 1, and from and including July 1 at a rate equal to the five-year U.S. Treasury rate plus 4.94% per annum.

The dividends will be payable in arrears on January 1, April 1, July 1 and October 1 of each year, commencing October 1, 2025.

The preferred stock is expected to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the ticker symbol MBNKO.

The underwriters have also been granted a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 450K shares of the series G preferred stock.

The net proceeds are intended to be used for general corporate purposes, which include increasing Medallion Bank’s capital level, growing the consumer loan portfolio and redeeming some or all of its outstanding series F preferred stock.

The offering is expected to close on May 22.

data.title: Applied Materials' outlook appears somewhat muted due to China trade issues: analysts
data.description:
Applied Materials' (NASDAQ:AMAT) second quarter financial results revealed strong demand trends in advanced logic and NAND, but exposure to China partially offsets its growth outlook, according to analysts.

Shares were down about 5% during early market trading on Friday. Competitors Lam Research (LRCX), KLA Corp. (KLAC) and ASML (ASML) were all down less than 1%.

"Applied materials delivered in-line Apr-Qtr revenue results, better GMs and slightly better EPS and provided an in-line (versus consensus) Jul-Qtr revenue/GM and slightly better EPS outlook reflecting strong spending trends in advanced foundry/logic/DRAM, technology transitions in NAND, partially offset by weak mature/lagging edge technology spending (primarily China which was down 21% Q/Q in the April-Qtr)," said J.P. Morgan analysts Harlan Sur and Peter Peng, in a Friday investor note.

J.P. Morgan maintained its Overweight rating but reduced its price target to $210 from $240.

"We see lower spending in China, with investments in both DRAM and mature logic down for the year," said Applied Material CEO Gary Dickerson, during the company's earnings call.

However, Applied Materials is increasing its sales in other regions.

"In 2024, we under-performed the market in China, due to the market access restrictions imposed on U.S. companies," Dickerson said. "At the same time, outside of China, we grew faster than our peer group thanks to our strength in leading edge foundry and DRAM. Trade restrictions have also had an impact on our service business. Despite these headwinds, we grew our core parts and services revenues in the low double-digit range last year, and we are on track to deliver a similar growth rate in 2025."

"We'll have a record next quarter, and we're saying for the whole year, even despite losing -- not being able to serve some of those accounts in China, we will have low double-digit growth in the core business, so without that 200-millimeter," said Applied Materials Chief Financial Officer Brice Hill.

"Despite robust trends in leading-edge F/L and advanced DRAM/HBM, AMAT’s higher trailing-edge exposure (we est. roughly 1/3 of overall sales vs. ~20-30% for peers) is a drag on growth in CY25 mainly tied to moderating China mix," said Bank of America Securities analysts, led by Vivek Arya, in a note.

BofA maintained its Buy rating and $190 price target.

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley reiterated its Underweight rating and slightly lowered its target to $162 from $164. Long term, the tariff and export control issues could be beneficial if it drives more multinationals to spend in the U.S.

"The US appears to be shifting to more stick than carrot when it comes to spending incentives - tariffs replacing subsidies - but particularly if phased in gradually those incentives could be significant," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Joseph Moore.

Finally, Needham maintained its Buy rating and $195 target. The investment bank pointed out Applied Materials' strong gross margin.

"Gross margin was 49.2%, a record high for the company," said Needham analysts Charles Shi and Ross Cole. "This is achieved when Taiwan revenue mix is high and China revenue mix is low, which in the prior years would have led to GM declines. Management also highlighted strength in process control also contributed to the GM strength, as PDC revenue reached a quarterly record, driven by leading-edge foundry/logic demand."

data.title: Bets against S&P 500 materials stocks ease in April, Albemarle most shorted
data.description:
Short bets against materials' stocks eased in April compared to the end of March, with Albemarle (ALB) once again topping the chart for the most shorted company, while Amcor (AMCR) took the second top spot.

DuPont de Nemours (DD) replaced Ecolab (ECL) as the least shorted in the pack.

The average short interest across S&P 500 materials stocks was 3.76% of the shares float at the end of April, down from 3.86% at the end of March.

The S&P 500’s materials sector (NYSEARCA:XLB) gained nearly 5% in the past month, compared to an over 8% rise in the broader market.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

 

 

Albemarle (ALB) had 17.06M shares sold short as of April 30, or 14.58% of the shares float.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings, the lithium miner has a Sell rating with a score of 1.62 out of 5.

"Albemarle's stock price decline offers a unique opportunity to invest in the EV trend at pre-boom levels, driven by a steep drop in lithium prices," one Seeking Alpha author writes.

“The steep decline in lithium prices and mining shares amounts to a second chance to invest in the EV trend as if it were in its infancy, because most indications are that the EV boom is set to continue, even as miners including Albemarle are cutting capital spending, setting the market up for renewed market tightness.”

Amcor (AMCR) meanwhile had 191.30M shares sold short at 8.34% of shares float, followed by International Paper (IP) and Celanese (CE) with short interest of 7.21% and 5.32%, respectively.

Turning to companies with the least short interest in the sector, DuPont de Nemours (DD), which has slipped over 7% this year, was preceded by Ecolab (ECL), with a short interest of 1.21% short interest.  

Industry Analysis

Containers & packaging remains the most shorted industry within the sector, although short interest as of the end of April came down to 6.71%, from 7.98% at the end of March.

Construction materials, which include Vulcan Materials (VMC) and Martin Marietta Materials (MLM), took the second spot with 2.80% short interest as of April 30, significantly up from 2.05% a month ago.

data.title: BlackRock sees bright spots as U.S.-China tariff deal eases trade tensions
data.description:
In its latest weekly market commentary report, BlackRock notes a significant step towards stabilizing global trade dynamics with the new U.S.-China deal, which marks a pivotal moment in the ongoing economic friction between the two countries.

The agreement, which includes a 90-day reduction in tariffs, offers clarity on future trade policy and supports a cautiously optimistic market outlook the asset manager said. According to BlackRock, the deal underscores three important insights. 

First, it reinforces the idea that firm economic principles—particularly the constraints of supply chains—are driving policy decisions. “The 90-day cut to U.S.-China tariffs shows a hard economic rule shaping policy: supply chains can’t be rewired quickly without disruption,” the firm stated. This suggests that both governments are beginning to prioritize economic fundamentals over political signaling. 

Second, while the temporary tariff relief may continue to cause short-term slowdowns in production and trade activity, BlackRock anticipates the overall economic effect in 2025 will be less severe than previously feared. The investment firm suggests that the intermittent supply shocks could still contribute to inflation and reduce growth in the near term but don’t necessarily spell a full-year contraction for the U.S. economy. “We still see supply disruptions upping inflation and hitting growth, but also a path to avoiding a U.S. contraction over 2025 as a whole,” BlackRock noted. 

Finally, the deal sheds light on the expected long-term trajectory of U.S. trade policy, particularly with regard to tariff levels. The effective tariff rate appears to be reaching a more stable zone, allowing markets and businesses to plan accordingly. BlackRock concluded its analysis with a cautiously bullish stance: “We stay risk on, monitoring corporate earnings reports for selective opportunities.”

Market Tracking ETFs: (NYSEARCA:DIA), (NYSEARCA:DDM), (NYSEARCA:DOG), (NYSEARCA:DXD), (NYSEARCA:SDOW), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), (NYSEARCA:SPXU), (NASDAQ:QQQ), (NASDAQ:QQQM), (NYSEARCA:QLD), (NASDAQ:TQQQ), (NYSEARCA:QID), and (NASDAQ:SQQQ).

data.title: Fortnite blocked on all iOS devices
data.description:
Fortnite, the popular multiplayer online shooting game, has been made unavailable by Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) following Epic Games' submission for the latest update that was initially put on hold for 24 hours by the tech giant.

"Apple has blocked our Fortnite submission so we cannot release to the US App Store or to the Epic Games Store for iOS in the European Union. Now, sadly, Fortnite on iOS will be offline worldwide until Apple unblocks it," according to an official statement on X on Friday.

The game was removed by Apple and Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) back in 2020 for guideline violation after Epic introduced a payment feature to bypass the 30% commission imposed by the companies.

But after a four-year hiatus, Fortnite was made available on iOS in the EU and on Android devices globally after Epic launched its games store. Epic said it would charge a store fee of 12% for payments processed and 0% on third-party payments. 

Earlier this year, Apple blocked Epic's developer account after losing faith in the game company's ability to fulfill contractual obligations and called them "verifiably untrustworthy" in a letter.

"In terminating Epic’s developer account, Apple is taking out one of the largest potential competitors to the Apple App Store," Epic had said then.

data.title: Fresnillo sells majority of MAG Silver shares as Pan American deal proceeds
data.description:
Fresnillo (OTCPK:FNLPF) said Friday it has sold the majority of its stake in MAG Silver (NYSE:MAG) following Pan American Silver’s (PAAS) agreement to acquire the Canadian miner, saying the resulting dilution of its holding no longer aligned with its strategic objectives.

Under the terms of the proposed merger, Fresnillo's (OTCPK:FNLPF) stake of more than 9% in MAG Silver (NYSE:MAG) would convert into less than 1% of Pan American (PAAS) shares.

Fresnillo (OTCPK:FNLPF) said its share sale did not reflect any change in its commitment to the Juanicipio mine in Mexico, which it continued to describe as a core asset and one of the world's premier silver mines where it is the operator and majority owner.

Proceeds from the share sale will be deployed according to Fresnillo's (OTCPK:FNLPF) capital priorities, including funding its development pipeline and strengthening its balance sheet, the company said.

On Sunday, Pan American Silver (PAAS) announced an agreement to buy MAG Silver (MAG) in a cash-and-stock deal valued at ~$2.1B; MAG silver shareholders would own ~14% of Pan American following the deal's completion.
